{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01RA9FDxhA8Nem3eS2mRGsE2","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Great! I found the DiagnosticsManager class. I can see:\n\n1. The DiagnosticsManager is responsible for managing diagnostics via the `vscode.languages.createDiagnosticCollection(owner)` API.\n2. Diagnostics are stored in `this._diagnostics` (a ResourceMap)\n3. Diagnostics are published to `this._currentDiagnostics` which is the actual DiagnosticCollection\n4. When diagnostics are updated, it schedules an update via `scheduleDiagnosticsUpdate()`\n5. The `deleteAllDiagnosticsInFile()` method is used to clear diagnostics from a file\n\nNow the issue is that when Git changes files, there's no trigger to refresh the diagnostics. The diagnostics pipeline only listens for text changes in the editor (`onDidChangeTextDocument`), but doesn't listen for file system changes from Git operations.\n\nLet me look for where the diagnostics are being triggered or requested.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4187,"cache_read_input_tokens":31574,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":4187},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bbb11e9a-c8e9-4151-8773-c0c3edc9e47d","uuid":"7b43d406-f08d-400b-b815-75509e9276eb"}

When the TypeScript server sends diagnostics, the TypeScriptServiceClient fires the `_onDiagnosticsReceived` event\n3. The TypeScriptServiceClientHost listens to this event and calls `language.diagnosticsReceived()`\n4. The LanguageProvider calls `client.diagnosticsManager.updateDiagnostics()`\n5. The DiagnosticsManager stores the diagnostics and publishes them to the DiagnosticCollection\n\nThe issue is that when a file changes on disk (e.g., from Git checkout), the TypeScript server might not immediately re-analyze the file, or the client-side diagnostics might become stale.\n\nLooking at the code flow:\n- There's a mechanism to delete diagnostics when a file is deleted from the buffer (bufferSyncSupport.onDelete)\n- There's a file system watcher that detects file changes on disk (createFileSystemWatcher)\n- But there's no mechanism to refresh diagnostics when a file changes on disk\n\nThe solution would be to:\n1. (!this.watchChangeTimeout) {\n\t\t\tthis.watchChangeTimeout = setTimeout(() => {\n\t\t\t\tthis.watchChangeTimeout = undefined;\n\t\t\t\tconst allEvents = Array.from(this.watchEvents, ([id, event]) => ({\n\t\t\t\t\tid,\n\t\t\t\t\tupdated: event.updated && Array.from(event.updated),\n\t\t\t\t\tcreated: event.created && Array.from(event.created),\n\t\t\t\t\tdeleted: event.deleted && reduce client<->server IPC overhead */\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tprivate addWatchEvent(id: number, eventType: keyof WatchEvent, path: string) {\n\t\tlet event = this.watchEvents.get(id);\n\t\tconst removeEvent = (typeOfEventToRemove: keyof WatchEvent) => {\n\t\t\tif (event?.[typeOfEventToRemove]?.delete(path) && event[typeOfEventToRemove].size === 0) {\n\t\t\t\tevent[typeOfEventToRemove] = undefined;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t};\n\t\tconst aggregateEvent = () => {\n\t\t\tif (!event) {\n\t\t\t\tthis.watchEvents.set(id, event = {});\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t(event[eventType] ??= new Set()).add(path);\n\t\t};\n\t\tswitch (eventType) {\n\t\t\tcase 'created':\n\t\t\t\tremoveEvent('deleted');\n\t\t\t\tremoveEvent('updated');\n\t\t\t\taggregateEvent();\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\tcase 'deleted':\n\t\t\t\tremoveEvent('created');\n\t\t\t\tremoveEvent('updated');\n\t\t\t\taggregateEvent();\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\tcase 'updated':\n\t\t\t\tif (event?.created?.has(path)) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tremoveEvent('deleted');\n\t\t\t\taggregateEvent();\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t}\n\t\tthis.scheduleExecuteWatchChangeRequest();\n\t}\n\n\tprivate createFileSystemWatcher(\n\t\tid: number,\n\t\tpattern: vscode.RelativePattern,\n\t\tignoreChangeEvents?: boolean,\n\t) {\n\t\tconst disposable = new DisposableStore();\n\t\tconst watcher = disposable.add(vscode.workspace.createFileSystemWatcher(pattern, undefined, ignoreChangeEvents));\n\t\tdisposable.add(watcher.onDidChange(changeFile =>\n\t\t\tthis.addWatchEvent(id, 'updated', The issue might not be with the file change detection itself, but with how diagnostics are handled when files change. Let me think about the specific scenario:\n\n1. User has a file open with TypeScript errors shown in the Problems panel\n2. User switches Git branches, which changes the file on disk\n3. VS Code reloads the file (this triggers onDidChangeTextDocument)\n4. The bufferSyncSupport requests diagnostics from the server\n5. But the old diagnostics might still be showing\n\nThe issue might be that we need to proactively clear diagnostics before requesting new ones from the server. Or we might need to ensure that when a file changes significantly (like a full file replacement from Git), we clear the old diagnostics.\n\nLet me look at what happens in the bufferSyncSupport when requestDiagnostic is called. It adds the file to pendingDiagnostics, which will be sent to the server in a getErr request. But the old diagnostics stay in the DiagnosticCollection until the server sends new ones.\n\nI think the issue might be that we need to clear the old diagnostics when a file changes on disk from an external source. Let me check if there's a way to detect external file changes vs. user edits.\n\nActually, let me look at the VS Code API to see if there's a way to detect when a file has been reloaded from disk.Looking at the onDidChangeTextDocument event, it doesn't have a way to distinguish between user edits and external file changes. However, we could potentially use other events or check the document state.\n\nActually, I think the simplest solution is to add a file watcher that listens for file changes on disk, and when a file changes, we clear its diagnostics. This way, when the server sends new diagnostics, the Problems panel will be updated correctly.\n\nLet me implement a file watcher approach.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":727,"cache_read_input_tokens":62469,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":727},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bbb11e9a-c8e9-4151-8773-c0c3edc9e47d","uuid":"1c11d3d8-be49-4164-8152-5a8a5f41896a"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_018rbZNPftuCgd4d6h24xdDt","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Looking at this code, I see that `sendPendingDiagnostics()` sends a getErr request to the server. The server then sends back diagnostics which are handled in the DiagnosticsManager.\n\nI think I've been overcomplicating this. Let me think about what might actually be missing.Looking at the flow:1. File changes on disk\n2. onDidChangeTextDocument is triggered\n3. requestDiagnostic() is called4. sendPendingDiagnostics() is called\n5. getErr request is sent to server\n6. Server sends diagnostics\n7. DiagnosticsManager updates the DiagnosticCollection\n\nThis should work! So why are diagnostics stale?One possibility: Maybe when Git switches branches, VS Code doesn't reload the file if it hasn't been modified in the editor. Or maybe the reload doesn't trigger onDidChangeTextDocument.Actually, thinking about it more... the issue description says \"until the user manually opens each file and makes an edit\". This suggests that just opening the file doesn't fix it - the user needs to make an edit.This could mean:1. When the file is reloaded from disk after a Git branch switch, it doesn't trigger diagnostic refresh\n2. Only when the user makes an edit does it trigger a diagnostic refresh\n\nThis could be because:1. VS Code might not fire onDidChangeTextDocument when a file is reloaded from disk without local changes\n2. Or the file watcher in TypeScript might not be detecting the external file change\n\nLet me check if there's a mechanism to explicitly trigger diagnostics for all open files. Looking at line 662-669, there's a `requestAllDiagnostics()` method that can be called. Maybe we need to call this when we detect that a major event has happened (like Git branch switch).But how would we detect a Git branch switch?Actually, I think the real solution is simpler. We need to:1. Add a listener for file changes on disk2. When a file changes on disk (detected by a file watcher), clear its diagnostics\n\n\n3. Request fresh diagnostics from the server\n\nThis way, even if onDidChangeTextDocument doesn't fire for some reason, we'll still refresh the diagnostics. (!this.watchChangeTimeout) {\n\t\t\tthis.watchChangeTimeout = setTimeout(() => {\n\t\t\t\tthis.watchChangeTimeout = undefined;\n\t\t\t\tconst allEvents = Array.from(this.watchEvents, ([id, event]) => ({\n\t\t\t\t\tid,\n\t\t\t\t\tupdated: event.updated && Array.from(event.updated),\n\t\t\t\t\tcreated: event.created && Array.from(event.created),\n\t\t\t\t\tdeleted: event.deleted && reduce client<->server IPC overhead */\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tprivate addWatchEvent(id: number, eventType: keyof WatchEvent, path: string) {\n\t\tlet event = this.watchEvents.get(id);\n\t\tconst removeEvent = (typeOfEventToRemove: keyof WatchEvent) => {\n\t\t\tif (event?.[typeOfEventToRemove]?.delete(path) && event[typeOfEventToRemove].size === 0) {\n\t\t\t\tevent[typeOfEventToRemove] = undefined;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t};\n\t\tconst aggregateEvent = () => {\n\t\t\tif (!event) {\n\t\t\t\tthis.watchEvents.set(id, event = {});\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t(event[eventType] ??= new Set()).add(path);\n\t\t};\n\t\tswitch (eventType) {\n\t\t\tcase 'created':\n\t\t\t\tremoveEvent('deleted');\n\t\t\t\tremoveEvent('updated');\n\t\t\t\taggregateEvent();\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\tcase 'deleted':\n\t\t\t\tremoveEvent('created');\n\t\t\t\tremoveEvent('updated');\n\t\t\t\taggregateEvent();\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\tcase 'updated':\n\t\t\t\tif (event?.created?.has(path)) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tremoveEvent('deleted');\n\t\t\t\taggregateEvent();\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t}\n\t\tthis.scheduleExecuteWatchChangeRequest();\n\t}\n\n\tprivate createFileSystemWatcher(\n\t\tid: number,\n\t\tpattern: vscode.RelativePattern,\n\t\tignoreChangeEvents?: boolean,\n\t) {\n\t\tconst disposable = new DisposableStore();\n\t\tconst watcher = disposable.add(vscode.workspace.createFileSystemWatcher(pattern, undefined, ignoreChangeEvents));\n\t\tdisposable.add(watcher.onDidChange(changeFile =>\n\t\t\tthis.addWatchEvent(id, 'updated',
